query_normalized: exploring the relationship between prime gaps and the riemann hypothesis mathematics
rationale: This paper belongs in the literature review because it addresses the Riemann Hypothesis and the statistical properties of the Riemann zeta function's zeros (the non-trivial zeros), which is the foundational domain and mechanism required to understand the relationship between prime gaps and the hypothesis (Category a and f).
